name: Implementation Intentions
tagline: Turn a vague goal into an automatic 'if X, then Y' plan.

origin.source: Gollwitzer, P.M. (1999). Implementation intentions. American Psychologist

situations: Goals that never translate into action
Forgetting to do the thing in the moment
Wanting to build or break a habit
summary: An implementation intention is a precommitted plan in the form 'If situation X arises, then I will do Y.' By deciding in advance exactly when, where, and how you'll act, you hand the decision to the environment instead of relying on in-the-moment willpower. It's one of the most reliably effective behavior-change techniques in psychology.
howItWorks: Goal intentions ('I want to exercise') leave a gap between intention and action that willpower has to bridge — and willpower is unreliable. Implementation intentions close that gap by linking a specific cue to a specific response, so the behavior becomes triggered automatically when the cue appears. The plan effectively delegates control to the situation, freeing you from having to decide each time.

steps.instruction: State the goal behavior you want to happen.
Identify a specific, reliable cue: a time, place, or preceding event.
Write it as 'If [cue], then I will [behavior].'
steps.note: Example: 'If it's 7am and I've made coffee, then I will write for 20 minutes.'

steps.instruction: Rehearse the if–then link a few times so the cue becomes a trigger.

whenToUse: When goals keep failing to become action
Building a new habit or breaking an old one
When you forget intentions in the moment
Anticipating a tempting or hard situation in advance
whenNotToUse: When the cue is too vague or rare to reliably fire
For goals you haven't actually decided you want
As a substitute for addressing deeper ambivalence about the goal
tips: Anchor the cue to something that already happens daily
Be concrete — 'after lunch' beats 'in the afternoon'
Add an if–then for obstacles too: 'If I feel like skipping, then I'll do just 2 minutes'
Stack new habits onto existing routines

evidence.summary: A meta-analysis of 94 studies found implementation intentions have a medium-to-large effect on goal attainment across many domains.
citations: Gollwitzer, P.M. & Sheeran, P. (2006). Implementation intentions and goal achievement: a meta-analysis. Advances in Experimental Social Psychology.
